summary: Effective error handling in Playwright leverages built-in tracing, retries, and web-first assertions to produce clear, actionable failure diagnostics without masking genuine bugs.
reasoning: Web-first assertions provide automatic retries with clear timeout error messages, reducing false negatives
Tracing captures full execution context (screenshots, network, DOM) making post-mortem debugging efficient
Swallowing errors with try/catch hides real failures and defeats the purpose of automated testing
Custom assertion messages make CI failure reports immediately actionable without requiring trace analysis
Retries handle transient infrastructure issues but should not be a substitute for fixing flaky test logic
expect.soft() enables comprehensive validation tests that report all failures rather than stopping at the first
